---
title: "common_3"
output: html_document
date: "2026-01-19"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
set.seed(42)
```

```{r}
#load the libraries
library(harmony)
library(dplyr)
library(openxlsx)

```

```{r}
# Assign sample labels to each singlet Seurat object prior to merging for downstream integration

obj1 <- third_singlets
obj2 <- second_singlets
obj3 <- first_singlets

obj1$sample <- "third"
obj2$sample <- "second"
obj3$sample <- "first"

```

```{r}
# Merge the three singlet datasets into one Seurat object for joint analysis
combined_JMML_AML_harmony <- merge(obj1, y = list(obj2, obj3), project = "JMML_AML_3samples")
```


```{r}
# Perform standard preprocessing on the merged dataset: normalization, variable feature selection,
# scaling, and PCA as input for Harmony-based batch correction
combined_JMML_AML_harmony <- NormalizeData(combined_JMML_AML_harmony)
combined_JMML_AML_harmony <- FindVariableFeatures(combined_JMML_AML_harmony, selection.method = "vst", nfeatures = 3000)
combined_JMML_AML_harmony <- ScaleData(combined_JMML_AML_harmony)
combined_JMML_AML_harmony <- RunPCA(combined_JMML_AML_harmony, npcs = 50, verbose = FALSE)

```


```{r}
# Check the batch effect

DimPlot(combined_JMML_AML_harmony, reduction = "pca", group.by = "sample")

```

```{r}
# Apply Harmony batch correction to remove sample-specific technical effects
# while preserving biological variation across the merged dataset

combined_JMML_AML_harmony <- harmony::RunHarmony(
  object = combined_JMML_AML_harmony,
  group.by.vars = "sample",
  reduction.use = "pca",
  dims.use = 1:30,
  verbose = TRUE
)

```

```{r}
# Perform UMAP visualization, construct neighbor graph, and identify clusters
# using Harmony-corrected embeddings of the integrated dataset

combined_JMML_AML_harmony <- RunUMAP(combined_JMML_AML_harmony, reduction = "harmony", dims = 1:30)
combined_JMML_AML_harmony <- FindNeighbors(combined_JMML_AML_harmony, reduction = "harmony", dims = 1:30)
combined_JMML_AML_harmony <- FindClusters(combined_JMML_AML_harmony, resolution = 1)

```


```{r}

# Visualize UMAP of the integrated Harmony-corrected dataset, coloring cells by Seurat cluster identities
DimPlot(combined_JMML_AML_harmony,
        reduction = "umap",
        group.by = "seurat_clusters",
        label = TRUE, repel = TRUE)


```


```{r}
# Generate a cluster-to-celltype mapping table by assigning each Seurat cluster
# to the most frequent cell type within that cluster, and export the table to Excel

cluster_to_celltype <- combined_JMML_AML_harmony@meta.data %>%
  count(seurat_clusters, celltype) %>%
  group_by(seurat_clusters) %>%
  slice_max(n, n = 1) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(as.numeric(seurat_clusters))


write.xlsx(cluster_to_celltype,
           file = "cluster_to_celltype_mapping.xlsx",
           rowNames = FALSE)
```



```{r}
# Try to identify clusters
DotPlot(combined_JMML_AML_harmony,
            features = c("CD14", "S100A9", "FCN1", "LGALS3"))
```


```{r}
# Refine cell type annotations in the integrated dataset by manually reassigning
# selected Seurat clusters to more specific biological identities

combined_JMML_AML_harmony$celltype_refined <- combined_JMML_AML_harmony$celltype

# cluster 11 → Monocytes
combined_JMML_AML_harmony$celltype_refined[
  combined_JMML_AML_harmony$seurat_clusters == "11"
] <- "Monocytes"

# cluster 20 → Megakaryblasts
combined_JMML_AML_harmony$celltype_refined[
  combined_JMML_AML_harmony$seurat_clusters == "20"
] <- "Megakaryblasts"

```


```{r}
# Visualize the integrated UMAP colored by refined cell type annotations
DimPlot(combined_JMML_AML_harmony,
        reduction = "umap",
        group.by = "celltype_refined",
        label = TRUE,
        repel = TRUE)


```

```{r}
# Create a refined cluster-to-celltype mapping table based on the updated annotations
# and export it to Excel for reporting

cluster_to_celltype_refined <- combined_JMML_AML_harmony@meta.data %>%
  count(seurat_clusters, celltype_refined) %>%
  group_by(seurat_clusters) %>%
  slice_max(n, n = 1) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(as.numeric(seurat_clusters))

write.xlsx(cluster_to_celltype_refined,
           file = "cluster_to_celltype_mapping_refined.xlsx",
           rowNames = FALSE)

```
